title: rosa glauca var. bellevallis
intro: Rosa glauca var. bellevallis, also known as the red-leaved rose, is a variety of rose plant that is native to the Mediterranean region and is prized for its distinctive reddish-purple foliage and fragrant white flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Rosa glauca var. bellevallis are believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. Rosa glauca var. bellevallis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in skincare products due to its astringent and anti-aging properties.
